Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, For Summarh Adjudication;
Off Calendar Jul-15-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC AND CHASE MANHATTAN BANK USA, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Appearances: Eugene Chiarelli of Chiarelli & Mollica (925-974-3325) for defendant Countrywide Home Loans, Inc.
S. Christopher Yoo of Adorno Yoss Alvarado & Smith (714-852-6800) for plaintiffs Chase Home Finance and Chase Manhattan Bank
John G. Hanlin of Hanlin Kostmayer & Associates (978-9580) for defendants Maria Sakurada and Francis Maka
Argued and the Court ruled as follows: Chase's motion for summary judgment is denied. Chase fails to shift the burden of producing evidence in that Chase has failed to demonstrate that Countrywide's deed of trust is not entitled to priority. First Fidelity Thrift & Loan Ass'n v. Alliance Bank (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1433, 1442. There is no evidence that Countrywide had knowledge of the alleged interest of Chase and Countrywide did not have an obligation to search the record to examine all documents recorded in connection with the subject property. In moving for summary judgment, Chase also failed to address Countrywide's fifth cause of action for equitable subrogation. Further, there is triable issue of fact based on the Sakurada testimony. As to Chase's motion for summary adjudication, the Court rules as follows: denied as to the first cause of action, quiet title, based on First Fidelity; denied as to causes of action two through seven - cancellation of written instruments, rescission and reformation - for Chase did not have a contractual relationship with Countrywide; denied as to the 8th and 9th causes of action - declaratory and injunctive relief - that are based on causes of action two through 7 in whole or in part; denied as directed to the 1st and 8th causes of action of Countrywide's First Amended Cross-Complaint - declaratory relief and quiet title - based on First Fidelity. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR #5162
